text:
🔭 Kiro.dev — Amazon's spec-driven agentic IDE

Amazon's Kiro (launched May 2026, replacing Amazon Q Developer) inverts the Cursor model: generates a spec bundle first — requirements.md + design.md + tasks.md — writes zero code until the spec is approved. Event-driven Hooks then auto-run tests, update docs, and cascade spec changes on file save/PR open.

Key points:
- Free tier: 500 bonus credits on first AWS Builder ID sign-in (30 days), no credit card [Fact]
- Uses Claude Sonnet for specs + Amazon Nova for codegen via Bedrock [Fact / AWS Builder Center]
- AWS-native depth (CDK, Lambda, IAM from spec) that Cursor/Windsurf lack [Fact]
- Pro: $20/mo — same price as Cursor, opposite philosophy [Fact]
- Hooks = trigger-action on code events; same philosophy as Personal OS skills/automations [Inference]
